title: MCP Security, Prompt Injection, and Supply Chain Trust · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Threat-model an MCP server consuming untrusted content and exposing write tools. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an MCP security test suite and operating policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect server provenance, dependencies, update channel, permissions, and data destinations.
Treat external resource and tool content as untrusted data that cannot redefine authority.
Bind actions to user intent, authorization, audience, and protected resource.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext mcp security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Security Operations trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for patch, rotate, revoke, audit, rate-limit, and incident-test mcp deployments.

content.text: For Security Operations, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Security Operations precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext mcp security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A secure launch configuration requires no ongoing operations.

stimulus.title: Security Operations case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: agent ext mcp security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A secure launch configuration requires no ongoing operations.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Patch, rotate, revoke, audit, rate-limit, and incident-test MCP deployments. Artifact under review: an MCP security test suite and operating policy.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Security Operations, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Security Operations applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Patch, rotate, revoke, audit, rate-limit, and incident-test MCP deployments.
